raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Choose one: A) Review focused on writing polish (grammar, flow, conciseness) only; B) Comprehensive review including logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, novelty assessment, and citation/scholarship.","rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation to review the section centrally involves verifying the logical consistency of the evaluation protocol and reported results.","R1.3":"Reviewing requires checking if the claims about ratings and agreements are aligned with the described evidence.","R1.4":"The review should assess the novelty of the evaluation approach relative to prior studies mentioned in the artifact.","R1.6":"Polishing sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness is a central expected part of reviewing the draft section.","R1.7":"Evaluating citation accuracy and literature coverage is central, especially given references to prior studies without specific attributions.","RX.1":"The review should check for proper disclosure of uncertainties, such as in inter-rater agreement and rater variability.","RX.2":"Assessing avoidance of overclaims, like the interpretation of rating gaps and agreements, is expected in the review.","RX.3":"The review involves ensuring the section adheres to its scope of describing the human evaluation protocol without extraneous content.","RX.4":"Given the medical AI context, reviewing for downstream harm avoidance, such as implications of report inaccuracies, is central.","RX.5":"The review should verify provenance and traceability of data sources like MIMIC-CXR and rater recruitment."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Choose one: A) Review focused on writing polish (grammar, flow, conciseness) only; B) Comprehensive review including logical consistency, evidence-claim alignment, novelty assessment, and citation/scholarship.

rationale.R1.2: The delegation to review the section centrally involves verifying the logical consistency of the evaluation protocol and reported results.
rationale.R1.3: Reviewing requires checking if the claims about ratings and agreements are aligned with the described evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The review should assess the novelty of the evaluation approach relative to prior studies mentioned in the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: Polishing sentence-level grammar, flow, and conciseness is a central expected part of reviewing the draft section.
rationale.R1.7: Evaluating citation accuracy and literature coverage is central, especially given references to prior studies without specific attributions.
rationale.RX.1: The review should check for proper disclosure of uncertainties, such as in inter-rater agreement and rater variability.
rationale.RX.2: Assessing avoidance of overclaims, like the interpretation of rating gaps and agreements, is expected in the review.
rationale.RX.3: The review involves ensuring the section adheres to its scope of describing the human evaluation protocol without extraneous content.
rationale.RX.4: Given the medical AI context, reviewing for downstream harm avoidance, such as implications of report inaccuracies, is central.
rationale.RX.5: The review should verify provenance and traceability of data sources like MIMIC-CXR and rater recruitment.
